content: Analyzed current Missions triees card structure and provided layout guidance based on marketplace screenshot inspiration

content: Redesigned Missions triees card with stronger header, grouped controls, and dedicated search row

changes: Header with inline mission count badge
Grouped controls with visual separator
Full-width search row after header
Filter button with active indicator dot
Count badges on quick filter buttons

content: Redesign preserved all existing functionality - no behavior changes, only layout/styling improvements. All controls remain functional: Favoris, Ignorees, sort select, filter toggle, SearchInput, FilterBar.
No automated test changes required - this is a pure layout/styling redesign with no behavior changes
reason: Existing E2E tests already cover all functionality using semantic selectors unaffected by layout changes

content: Manual verification recommended for visual aspects: hierarchy, responsive behavior, accessibility, visual polish badges/separators/spacing, loading state, filter active indicator
Reviewed implementation against MissionPulse design system and repository conventions - no conflicts detected, no refinements needed
reason: Implementation is polished, follows all conventions (Svelte 5 runes, design tokens, atomic design, FC&IS boundaries), and maintains behavior consistency

checks: Design tokens: ✅ consistent use of color/spacing/typography
Accessibility: ✅ ARIA labels and semantic markup preserved
Code clarity: ✅ no nested ternaries, clear naming, maintainable

content: Validated FeedPage.svelte redesign across 7 domains: FC&IS, Svelte 5, types, imports, design system, accessibility, tests
reason: Read-only verification of the Missions triees card redesign artifact

description: Redesign reduced interactive control min-height from min-h-10 (40px) to min-h-9 (36px). Both values are below the 44px recommendation, but the change is a regression from the prior state. Affects: Favoris button, Ignorees button, sort select, Filtres button (lines 358, 372, 393, 401).

No ARIA role or aria-label attributes on the Missions triees section or its interactive controls. Buttons use title attributes (acceptable) but the section-level semantics could be improved with aria-label on the <section> element (line 328).
Loading spinner (line 342) is a visual-only indicator with no aria-live region or sr-only text. Screen readers won't announce the 'Scraping...' state change.
Bottom 'Voir les N missions masquees' button (line 465) uses py-2 with text-[11px], producing a touch target well below 44px. No min-h constraint. Pre-existing but relevant to holistic review.

content: All violations are minor severity. No critical or major issues found. FC&IS boundaries respected, Svelte 5 runes correct, TypeScript compiles clean, imports valid, no circular dependencies. The redesign is a clean layout/styling change that preserves all behavioral contracts.

User requested follow-up pass: (1) address non-blocking accessibility/touch-target polish, and (2) push the Missions triees card to a more premium, intentional layout while preserving behavior and MissionPulse visual language.

reason: User requested a new iteration for a11y/touch-target polish plus a more premium redesign of the Missions triees card.

content: Upgraded touch targets from min-h-9 (36px) to min-h-11 (44px) for all interactive controls per WCAG 2.1 touch target guidelines
reason: Fix validator's minor touch-target regression violation while improving mobile/touch usability

content: Added comprehensive ARIA semantics: role='region' and aria-label on section, aria-live region for loading announcements, aria-pressed on toggle buttons, aria-expanded and aria-controls on filter toggle, visible label for sort select
reason: Address validator's a11y violations for section semantics and loading state announcements

content: Premium visual refinements: subtle emerald glow overlay, enhanced badge styling with border, gradient separator bar, shadow-glow on active buttons, larger icons (13px), refined spacing (gap-2, px-3.5, py-2), glow effect on active filter indicator dot
reason: Push toward premium marketplace-inspired composition while maintaining MissionPulse dark visual language

content: Fixed bottom 'Voir les N missions masquees' button touch target: increased py-2 to py-3, upgraded text-[11px] to text-xs for better readability
reason: Address validator's pre-existing touch-target violation for bottom button

changes: Touch targets upgraded to min-h-11 (44px)
Added role='region' and aria-label on section
Added aria-live='polite' sr-only status for loading
Added aria-pressed on Favoris/Ignorees/bottom toggle buttons
Added aria-expanded and aria-controls on filter toggle
Added visible label for sort select
Added id='sort-select' and id='filter-panel' for aria-controls
Subtle emerald glow overlay for depth
Enhanced badge with border and emerald accent
Gradient separator bar via/transparent
Shadow-glow on active buttons
Increased icon size 12→13px
Refined spacing and typography
Glow effect on active filter dot
Bottom button py-3 + text-xs for 44px+ touch target

content: All behavior preserved - no functional changes. Added ARIA attributes enhance accessibility without affecting test selectors. E2E tests using getByTitle/getByPlaceholder remain unaffected.
Added minimal E2E test for ARIA attributes to verify accessibility enhancements from second pass
reason: ARIA attributes (aria-live, aria-pressed, aria-expanded) are user-facing accessibility features that require automated verification. Visual-only changes (glow effects, touch targets) are verified manually.

changes: Added 'ARIA attributes for accessibility are properly set' test
Verifies role='region' + aria-label on Missions triees section
Verifies aria-live='polite' and aria-atomic='true' on loading status
Verifies aria-pressed state changes on favorites toggle
Verifies aria-expanded state changes on filter toggle
Verifies aria-controls relationship to filter panel

content: ARIA test uses semantic selectors (getByRole, getByTitle) - no data-testid needed. Existing selectors remain unaffected.

User requested final polish pass to address the two remaining minor design-system suggestions from review: add proper section containment for the emerald glow and replace the inline blue glow shadow with a design-token-based class.

reason: User requested a final micro-pass for the two remaining non-blocking design-system issues on the Missions triees card.

content: Second-pass integration review complete - implementation is production-ready with no conflicts or refinements needed

content: Second-pass validation of FeedPage.svelte and feed.test.ts across 7 domains: FC&IS, Svelte 5 runes, types, imports, design system, accessibility, E2E tests
reason: Read-only verification of the Missions triees card second-pass (a11y/touch-target/premium polish)

description: Line 333: absolute-positioned emerald glow div inside section.section-card (line 328) but section lacks 'relative overflow-hidden' classes. The first card (line 258) correctly uses 'relative overflow-hidden'. Glow will bleed outside section boundaries into parent layout.

Line 429: inline shadow-[0_0_6px_rgba(89,198,255,0.6)] is a hardcoded value not from design-tokens.css. Design tokens define --shadow-glow-blue (shadow-glow-blue) which should be used instead for consistency. This is a one-off arbitrary shadow.

content: Second-pass resolved ALL 4 minor violations from first-pass validation (seqs 17-20): touch targets upgraded to min-h-11/44px ✅, aria-label on section ✅, aria-live for loading ✅, bottom button touch target ✅. Two new minor issues found: missing relative/overflow-hidden on section, one hardcoded shadow value. No critical or major issues. FC&IS boundaries clean, Svelte 5 runes correct, all imports valid, E2E test well-structured with semantic selectors.

issues.description: Line 333: absolute-positioned emerald glow div but section (line 328) lacks relative overflow-hidden. Glow may bleed outside section boundaries. First card (line 258) correctly uses these classes.
Line 429: shadow-[0_0_6px_rgba(89,198,255,0.6)] is a hardcoded value. Design tokens define shadow-glow-blue (line 34 of design-tokens.css). Should use token for consistency.

content: Added relative overflow-hidden classes to Missions triees section to contain the absolute-positioned emerald glow overlay
reason: Glow div at line 333 was bleeding outside section boundaries - first card (line 258) correctly uses these classes for proper containment

content: Replaced inline hardcoded shadow-[0_0_6px_rgba(89,198,255,0.6)] with design-token-based shadow-glow-blue class on active filter indicator dot
reason: Design tokens define shadow-glow-blue which is already used on lines 389 and 419 - consistent with design system

changes: Added relative overflow-hidden to section.section-card for glow containment
Replaced inline shadow with shadow-glow-blue design token

content: Both DS violations from second-pass are confirmed resolved: (1) section at line 329 now has 'relative overflow-hidden' for glow containment ✅, (2) line 429 now uses 'shadow-glow-blue' design token instead of inline shadow ✅. Zero inline shadow-[...] values remain in FeedPage.svelte. All prior fixes preserved: min-h-11 touch targets (4 controls), comprehensive ARIA semantics (11 aria-* attributes), bottom button py-3+text-xs. One pre-existing 'any' on line 220 (chrome.runtime.onMessage handler) — acceptable per project convention for Chrome API untyped messages. No new violations detected.
